mnemonics: Think of a fork in a blockchain's road: a SOFT fork is like adding a new lane (backward compatible), while a HARD fork is like building a completely new road (not compatible).
keyPoints: A fork is a change in the blockchain's protocol that creates two separate versions of the chain.
A soft fork is a backward-compatible update where the new rules are a subset of the old rules; non-upgraded nodes can still validate new blocks.
A hard fork is a permanent divergence from the previous version of the blockchain; it is not backward-compatible and requires all nodes to upgrade to the new protocol.
commonMistakes: Believing a soft fork does not require any nodes to update (miners must update to validate new rules).
Thinking a hard fork always results in two competing cryptocurrencies (it can be planned and agreed upon, resulting in only one chain).
